mnemonics: Meet Monte Carlo, Never Ever Break
keyPoints: Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) is a method for sampling from complex distributions.
Gaussian random numbers are used to sample from distributions with a known mean and variance.
Monte Carlo simulations can be used to estimate the behavior of complex systems.
The proposal distribution is used to perturb the current state in MCMC.
Markov Chains (MC) are used in MCMC to create a new sample state.
